dialogue.text: The backyard smells like cut grass and the last hour of sunlight. The kind of evening that doesn't know it's ending.

dialogue.speaker: drew
dialogue.text: Alright, listen up. I've got a plan for tonight and it involves all of you, so nobody bail on me.

dialogue.speaker: yen
dialogue.text: What kind of plan?

dialogue.speaker: drew
dialogue.text: We're sleeping out here. All four of us. Under the stars. Like we used to.

dialogue.speaker: rush
dialogue.text: We haven't done that since—

dialogue.speaker: drew
dialogue.text: Since we were little. Yeah. That's the point.
Drew looks at each of you. There's something in his eyes you can't quite name. Not sadness. Something heavier than that. Something that knows.

dialogue.speaker: lu
dialogue.text: I'm in. But only if we build a real fort this time. Not that sad pile of blankets from last year.

dialogue.speaker: drew
dialogue.text: Deal. Rush — you're on supplies. Lu — architecture. Yen...

dialogue.speaker: yen
dialogue.text: Snacks?

dialogue.speaker: drew
dialogue.text: Snacks.
Everyone laughs. It's easy. It's effortless. You'll remember this sound for the rest of your life.
You scatter to gather supplies. The sun drops lower. The shadows get longer.
The fort is a ridiculous masterpiece. Blankets draped over lawn chairs, held up by broom handles and optimism. Lu's design. It shouldn't work, but it does.

dialogue.speaker: drew
dialogue.text: Okay. Before we lose the light... I want to say something.
He doesn't look at anyone. He looks up.

dialogue.speaker: drew
dialogue.text: Whatever happens next... whatever goes sideways... you three stick together. You hear me?

dialogue.speaker: rush
dialogue.text: What are you talking about?

dialogue.speaker: drew
dialogue.text: Just... promise me.
The air changes. The crickets seem louder. Something about the way he says it makes the whole evening go still.

choices.notification: Drew smiles. But his eyes don't.

dialogue.speaker: drew
dialogue.text: Good.
He lays back. Hands behind his head. Looks at the stars like he's memorizing them.

dialogue.speaker: yen
dialogue.text: Drew? What's that?
At the edge of the yard, just past where the light reaches, something moves. Two eyes. Yellow-green. Watching.
A black cat steps into the lantern light. It's small. Impossibly still. It looks directly at Drew. Then at you.
The cat doesn't move the way cats move. It's too deliberate. Too patient. Like it chose this exact moment to arrive.

dialogue.speaker: lu
dialogue.text: It's just a stray. Shoo.

dialogue.speaker: kimiru
dialogue.text: I'm not stray. And I don't shoo.
Nobody breathes.

dialogue.speaker: yen
dialogue.text: The... the cat just...

dialogue.speaker: kimiru
dialogue.text: Spoke. Yes. You'll adjust.

dialogue.speaker: drew
dialogue.text: Okay. Either I'm losing it, or—

dialogue.speaker: kimiru
dialogue.text: You're not losing anything. Not yet.
The cat — Kimiru, though you don't know his name yet — sits. Wraps his tail around his paws. Precise. Like everything about him is measured.
He looks at Drew for a long time. Longer than is comfortable. Then he looks away.

choices.notification: Kimiru tilts his head. Almost approving.

choices.text: Drew... do you see this too?

dialogue.speaker: kimiru
dialogue.text: My name is Kimiru. I've been waiting.

dialogue.speaker: rush
dialogue.text: Waiting for what?

dialogue.speaker: kimiru
dialogue.text: For it to begin.
He says nothing else. The lantern flickers. Drew pulls Yen closer. Lu crosses her arms. Rush doesn't look away from the cat.
Somewhere, something shifts. The good day ends. Not with a crash. With a whisper.
